evals.prompt: Design a hero section for a developer tools landing page. Give me the Tailwind CSS + React code. Make it look premium and distinctive.
evals.expected_output: A React component with Tailwind CSS that avoids generic AI aesthetics — uses distinctive fonts (not Inter/Arial/Roboto), bold color choices, intentional spatial composition, and production-grade animation.

assertions.text: The code does not use Inter, Arial, Roboto, Open Sans, or system-ui as the primary font. Uses a distinctive typeface like Clash Display, Satoshi, Cabinet Grotesk, or similar.
The layout is not a simple centered text block with a button below it — it uses intentional spatial composition (asymmetry, overlapping elements, or creative grid usage).
The component includes at least one animation or motion effect (CSS transitions, Framer Motion, or similar) for entrance or interaction.
The component uses CSS variables or Tailwind theme tokens (bg-background, text-foreground, etc.) rather than hardcoded colors, supporting dark mode.

evals.prompt: I need a pricing card component in React + Tailwind. Three tiers: Free, Pro, Enterprise. Make it visually interesting — not the same boring pricing grid everyone else has.
evals.expected_output: A pricing component that breaks from the standard three-equal-columns layout. Uses distinctive typography, creative visual hierarchy to highlight the recommended tier, and avoids the generic AI pricing card pattern.

assertions.text: The recommended/popular tier is visually distinct from the others — not just a colored border but a genuinely different treatment (size, position, elevation, or background).
The layout does something more interesting than three identical-width columns side by side — uses creative sizing, stacking, or spatial relationships.
The component uses weight extremes (100-200 vs 700-900) or large size jumps (3x+) for typographic contrast, not subtle 400 vs 600 weight differences.
